Apple scab.symptoms: Olive-green to black velvety spots on leaves and fruit; leaves may yellow and drop early.
Apple scab.cause: Fungus Venturia inaequalis, favored by cool wet spring weather.
Apple scab.treatment: Remove and destroy fallen infected leaves; apply a scab fungicide from bud break.
Apple scab.prevention: Plant resistant cultivars, prune for airflow, rake leaf litter in autumn.

Apple rust.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface; tube-like structures underneath.
Apple rust.cause: Cedar-apple rust fungus needing both apple and nearby juniper/cedar hosts.
Apple rust.treatment: Apply a preventive fungicide at pink bud stage; remove nearby galls on junipers.
Apple rust.prevention: Avoid planting apples near junipers; choose rust-resistant varieties.

Corn leaf blight.symptoms: Long grey-green to tan cigar-shaped lesions that enlarge and merge.
Corn leaf blight.cause: Northern corn leaf blight fungus, favored by warm humid conditions.
Corn leaf blight.treatment: Apply a foliar fungicide at early onset; remove heavily infected debris.
Corn leaf blight.prevention: Rotate crops, use resistant hybrids, bury residue after harvest.

Corn gray leaf spot.symptoms: Rectangular grey to tan lesions bounded by leaf veins.
Corn gray leaf spot.cause: Fungus Cercospora zeae-maydis, worse in no-till humid fields.
Corn gray leaf spot.treatment: Apply fungicide if it appears before tasseling on susceptible hybrids.
Corn gray leaf spot.prevention: Rotate away from corn, till residue, plant resistant hybrids.

Corn rust.symptoms: Small cinnamon-brown raised pustules on both leaf surfaces.
Corn rust.cause: Common rust fungus Puccinia sorghi, favored by cool moist weather.
Corn rust.treatment: Fungicide rarely needed; apply on susceptible sweet corn if severe.
Corn rust.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids and avoid prolonged leaf wetness.

Tomato early blight.symptoms: Dark concentric target rings on older leaves with yellow halos.
Tomato early blight.cause: Fungus Alternaria solani, spread by water splash in warm humid weather.
Tomato early blight.treatment: Remove affected lower leaves and apply a labeled fungicide on schedule.
Tomato early blight.prevention: Mulch soil, stake plants, water at the base, rotate crops.

Tomato late blight.symptoms: Large greasy grey-green blotches turning brown; white mold underneath in humidity.
Tomato late blight.cause: Oomycete Phytophthora infestans, spreads fast in cool wet weather.
Tomato late blight.treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately; protectant fungicide on healthy ones.
Tomato late blight.prevention: Use disease-free transplants, space plants, avoid overhead watering.

Tomato septoria leaf spot.symptoms: Many small circular spots with dark borders and grey centers on lower leaves.
Tomato septoria leaf spot.cause: Fungus Septoria lycopersici, favored by wet foliage.
Tomato septoria leaf spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves, apply fungicide, avoid wetting foliage.
Tomato septoria leaf spot.prevention: Rotate crops, mulch, improve air circulation.

Tomato bacterial spot.symptoms: Small dark water-soaked spots on leaves; scabby spots on fruit.
Tomato bacterial spot.cause: Bacteria (Xanthomonas), spread by splashing water and handling.
Tomato bacterial spot.treatment: Apply copper-based sprays early; remove badly infected plants.
Tomato bacterial spot.prevention: Use clean seed, avoid working with wet plants, rotate.

Tomato leaf mold.symptoms: Pale yellow patches on top with olive-green velvety mold underneath.
Tomato leaf mold.cause: Fungus Passalora fulva, common in humid greenhouses.
Tomato leaf mold.treatment: Increase ventilation, lower humidity, apply a labeled fungicide.
Tomato leaf mold.prevention: Space plants, ventilate well, grow resistant varieties.

Tomato mosaic virus.symptoms: Mottled light-and-dark green leaves, often distorted.
Tomato mosaic virus.cause: Tobacco/tomato mosaic virus, spread by handling and tools.
Tomato mosaic virus.treatment: No cure; remove and destroy infected plants to limit spread.
Tomato mosaic virus.prevention: Wash hands and tools, control weeds, use resistant varieties.

Tomato yellow leaf curl virus.symptoms: Upward curling, yellowing, and stunting of young leaves.
Tomato yellow leaf curl virus.treatment: No cure; remove infected plants and control whiteflies.
Tomato yellow leaf curl virus.prevention: Use whitefly screens, reflective mulch, resistant varieties.

Tomato spider mites.symptoms: Fine yellow stippling and faint webbing; leaves bronze and dry.
Tomato spider mites.cause: Two-spotted spider mites, worse in hot dry conditions.
Tomato spider mites.treatment: Spray with water, apply insecticidal soap or miticide, remove infested leaves.
Tomato spider mites.prevention: Keep plants watered, avoid dust, encourage predatory mites.

Potato early blight.symptoms: Concentric dark target-spot lesions on older leaves.
Potato early blight.treatment: Apply fungicide at first symptoms and remove infected foliage.
Potato early blight.prevention: Rotate crops, fertilize adequately, avoid overhead irrigation.

Potato late blight.symptoms: Dark water-soaked lesions that rot leaves and stems rapidly.
Potato late blight.treatment: Destroy infected plants and apply protectant fungicide to the rest.
Potato late blight.prevention: Plant certified seed, hill soil over tubers, avoid wet foliage.

Grape black rot.symptoms: Brown circular leaf spots with dark borders; fruit shrivels into black mummies.
Grape black rot.cause: Fungus Guignardia bidwellii, favored by warm wet weather.
Grape black rot.treatment: Apply fungicide from early shoot growth; remove mummified berries.
Grape black rot.prevention: Prune for airflow, remove mummies, manage canopy.

Squash powdery mildew.symptoms: White powdery patches on leaves that yellow and dry.
Squash powdery mildew.cause: Powdery mildew fungi, favored by warm days and humid nights.
Squash powdery mildew.treatment: Apply sulfur, potassium bicarbonate, or a labeled fungicide; remove worst leaves.
Squash powdery mildew.prevention: Space plants, ensure sun and airflow, grow resistant varieties.

Bell pepper bacterial spot.symptoms: Small dark raised spots with yellow halos; leaf drop in severe cases.
Bell pepper bacterial spot.cause: Bacteria (Xanthomonas), spread by water and seed.
Bell pepper bacterial spot.treatment: Apply copper sprays early and remove infected debris.
Bell pepper bacterial spot.prevention: Use clean seed, rotate crops, avoid overhead watering.

Healthy leaf.symptoms: Uniform green color, no spots, lesions, mold, or distortion.
Healthy leaf.prevention: Keep monitoring, water at the base, maintain airflow and nutrition.
